dataset: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program - California Region

pi_items.source: Task description, setup_task.sh line 27, confirmed in export_result.sh

Live USGS API data (verified 2026-03-07): min magnitude 2.50. Fallback embedded data: min magnitude 2.5. IDW interpolation mathematically cannot produce values below minimum input value. Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inverse_distance_weighting (IDW output is bounded by input data range due to weighted averaging property).

Live USGS API data (verified 2026-03-07): max magnitude 4.26 from 129 earthquakes. Fallback embedded data: max magnitude 5.1. IDW interpolation mathematically cannot produce values above maximum input value. Reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inverse_distance_weighting (IDW is exact interpolator bounded by input extremes).

Calculated estimate: 0.05 degree pixel size over 10°×11° extent = ~200×220 pixels = 44,000 pixels. Float32 data: 176 KB minimum. With GeoTIFF headers: 200-300 KB expected. 5 KB is implausibly small for this raster configuration. No authoritative documentation found for QGIS IDW minimum file size requirement.

privileged_info_summary: The input data is real earthquake magnitude data from the USGS Earthquake Hazards Program API for the California region (latitude 32-42°, longitude -125 to -114°, magnitude threshold 2.5+). Live API data (verified 2026-03-07) contains 129 earthquakes with magnitudes ranging from 2.50 to 4.26. Fallback embedded data contains real earthquake data with magnitudes 2.5-5.1. IDW interpolation produces a weighted average bounded by the minimum (2.5) and maximum (4.26-5.1) input values—it cannot produce values of 0.0 or 10.0 as claimed in metadata. The expected output is a GeoTIFF raster file saved to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/earthquake_magnitude_surface.tif, with a companion QGIS project file (earthquake_interpolation.qgz).
